rulings.Case Title: SABEER BHATIA, ET. AL. VS. EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Charging Judgment Debtors Partnership Interest
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, August 10, 2015, Line 1, CROSS-COMPLAINANT SABEER BHATIA's Motion For Order Charging Judgment Debtors Partnership Interest The motion is continued to October 28, 2015, to give Judge Lee an opportunity to make her final rulings in the related consolidated case of CGC-09-488802 (O'Suillebhain v. Alac Dean Construction). The court enjoins Mr. Singha from transferring any interest he has in the following entities until further order of the court: (1) Yetizen Innovation Lab, LLC; (2) 1275 Calhoun, LLC; (3) Union Square Investments, LLC; and (4) American Karma, LLC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES SUSSNA VS. PRIME OLD COUNTY LP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Frances Sussnas Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Continued Deposition Of Prime Old County L.P. And Prime Old County Acquisition, Llcs Person Most Knowledgeable, Further Responses And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 10, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA'S Motion To Compel Continued Deposition Of Prime Old County L.P. And Prime Old County Acquisition, LLC's Person Most Knowledgeable, Further Responses And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Law & Equity To: 1. Vacate Clerk'S Entry Of Default Entered In Violation Of Ccp 585(D); Procured By Fraud; 2. Order To Vacate Order Of Judge Pro Tem Paul A. Renne As In Excess Of The Court'S Jurisdiction; 3. Order Vacating The Judgment By Stipulation
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, August 10, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT JOHN MILLER's Motion In Law & Equity To: 1. Vacate Clerk'S Entry Of Default Entered In Violation Of Ccp 585(D); Procured By Fraud; 2. Order To Vacate Order Of Judge Pro Tem Paul A. Renne As In Excess Of The Court'S Jurisdiction; 3. Order Vacating The Judgment By Stipulation The court denies the motion. Whether the default was void or properly entered is irrelevant/moot. The parties subsequently entered into a negotiated settlement/stipulation to resolve the case. Taintor v. Sup. Crt. (1949) 95 Cal.App.2d 346 does not preclude parties from settling a case after a default and there is a strong policy in favor of encouraging settlement. There is no basis to vacate the discovery order and plaintiff properly cites SF local rule 10B. Defendant stipulated to the discovery Judge Pro tem. Moreover, the enforceability of the discovery order is irrelevant/moot based on the settlement. Defendant fails to demonstrate "duress" to vacate the stipulation/settlement. The duress was self-created by the failure to comply with discovery mandates. In addition, there was nothing stopping defendant from seeking relief from the discovery sanctions. There is no authority holding that a settlement must be global to be enforceable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA HAMMES et al VS. HAIGHT & FILLMORE ASSOCIATES LP et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 10, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiffs Barbara Hammes, Alyssa Landy, and Noelle Narez's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories To Russell Flynn, Sets One And Two; Request For Sanctions Of $1,200.00 Against Defendant And His Counsel Of Record Catherine Golden. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Based upon the moving party's reply brief, it appears that the only remaining Interrogatories in dispute are Nos. 1, 37, 38, and 39. The motion is granted as to Interrogatory Nos. 1 and 37 and denied as to Nos. 38 and 39. Supplemental responses to be served within 20 days. The request for sanctions is denied.
